entry.title: Re: Sap IBP testing
entry.content: Tag for test automation tool for SAP S/4HANA Cloud is not correct here as you are using SAP S/4HANA or IBP products

entry.title: Re: SAP User Experience News for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Q4/2023

entry.title: Design Your Site With the New SAP Build Work Zone Experience last step not working
entry.content:
i followed this tutorial step by step but cant get the descriped functionality out of the last step. it just shows the blank mainpage that was already available.

Does anyone have a solution or idea why this doesnt work as described?

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content:
In OACT you need to use storage category DMS and probably to recreate in OAC0 another content server, for DMS, pointing to the same content server as your ARCHIVELINK one.

Then use that new content server in OACT for DMS.

You'll have to distinct content servers in OAC0, one for Archivelink, one for DMS, but physically they will be the same server.

entry.title: Re: How is the "Missing Hours" is being calculated in "Ma...
entry.content:
Hi Evans,

 

The logic for calculation of missing hours in the 'Manage My Timesheet' tile is.

Prerequisites:

  1. Availability Data (Target Hours that user has to be maintained for the Day).
  2. Timesheet Data (Recorded Hours that user recorded their real working hours for the Day)
  3. Duration which limit the past days for Missing Hours' calculation (This is in the configuration "Maintain Data Entry Profiles" for Missing Days - Default is 60 Days)
    The user can also configure the Missing Days by themself.
    Note: Current day hours are not considered for the Missing Hours.

Calculation is done as follows: 

  1. Duration days for calculating the missing hours is (Current date – 1 ) - Past 60 days or the configured missing days.
  2. During the duration, if Recorded Hours is less than Target Hours then it is considered as Missing Day.
    Missing Hours for that day = Target Hours - Recorded Hours.
  3. Current logic calculates and sums up the number of Missing Hours for each day.
    Finally, the total missing hours are shown within the app.

In case the user wants to check the Target Hours and Recorded Hours:

  1. Target Hours: Can be checked from CDS view I_TIMEOVERVIEWBYWRKAGRMTANDDTE.
  2. Recorded Hours: Can be checked from CDS view I_TIMESHEETRECORD.

 

Regards,

Akshay

entry.title: Re: S/4HANA service - requote a service quation with refe...
entry.content:
Hi Abhishek,
I don't want to answer for Vojtech, but I also have a requirement for requoting in the context of advanced exection.
Scenario is as follows:
1. We quote to service or repair a customer item. As this involves a lot of work steps and components, we use the advanced execution scenario.
2. the customer accepts the quote and we start the work.
3. As we proceed with the work, we discover additional defects on the object that need to be addressed, but which were not visible at the time as they only become apparent once we dismantled the object.
4. We now want to amend the original quote with the additinal items, and send the customer an updated quote.
5. If the customner accepts these items, we want this to be part of the existing maintenance order. This is a key point for us as this is still part of the same business transaction and its part of the same service job.

From what I can tell, you can only do a requote with the advanced execution scenario by creating a new quoation item with a new and seperate maintenance order. We want to requote using the existing quotation number and the existing PM order number.
Is there a way to achieve this?

Many thanks

Patrick

entry.title: Need Ability to Apply Different User Statuses to Multiple WBS Elements Simultaneously in SAP
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am currently facing a limitation where user statuses can only be added to project briefs and are applied generally across the entire project structure. This restricts the flexibility to assign different user statuses to individual WBS elements within the same project.

In production lines, especially for tank manufacturing, having specific statuses for different activities is crucial for efficient project control. For example, in a tank production line, two tasks like bending sheet metal and welding can occur simultaneously, but each task requires a different user status to track its progress accurately.

Is there a way to configure SAP to allow different user statuses to be applied to multiple WBS elements simultaneously, based on the specific needs of each element?

Any insights or recommendations would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you in advance!

entry.title: Re: Edit BPMN Diagrams to adjust SAP Best Practice and Reference Processes in SAP Cloud ALM
entry.content: No Copy button available to Edit the Diagrams.

entry.title: Best Practices for Renumbering Accounts in YCOA and Chart of Accounts Management
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

I am looking for guidance on best practices for managing the chart of accounts, specifically regarding renumbering the necessary accounts.

Is the YCOA (Uniform Chart of Accounts) the mandatory operational plan, or is it possible to implement renumbering as needed? If renumbering is allowed, are there any specific strategies or recommended practices to follow in order to avoid disruptions in financial reporting and integration with other modules?

I would appreciate any advice or experiences on how to best approach this process.

Thanks in advance!

Bea

entry.title: How to retrieve data from a fieldymbol nested of deep structures
entry.content:
Hi *.

I am just working on nested dynamic structures as output of a JSON deserializsation.

At the moment I don't know how to retrieve the data from this nested dynamic structure as I don't see how to get down to the lowest level of the structure. Perhaps you have an idea?

Is start with lo_json_data as output of a deserialization method defined as type ref to data.

 

lo_json_data type ref to data.

field-symbols: <data>    type any,
               <objects> type any.

assign lo_json_data->* to <data>.

 

I can assign the reference of lo_json_data to a field-symbol <data> of type any to just have a look on the data structure.

As I know the structure of the JSON-File

 

{
  "objects":[
    {
      "object":{
         "properties":{
            "CbaseTypeId":{
            "id":"CbaseTypeId",
            "type":"id",
            "cardinality":"single",
            "value":"Cdocument"
            },
            "%CobjectTypeId":{
            "id":"%CobjectTypeId!,

            ...
            
            "%CcontentStreamDataLength":{
            "id":"%CcontentStreamDataLength",
            "type":"integer",
            "cardinality":"single",
            "value":47336
            }
          }
     },
     "pathSegment":"data"
     }
   ],
   "hasMoreItems":false
}

 

I can also define the (hopefully) corresponding ABAP based structure to be read bottom up:

 

types:
  begin of ty_table_line,
    tline type string,
  end of ty_table_line.

types:
  begin of ty_basetype,
    id          type string,
    type        type string,
    cardinality type string,
    value       type standard table of table_line with empty key,
  end of ty_basetype .

types:
  begin of ty_property,
    propertyname type string,
    property     type standard table of ty_basetype with empty key,
  end of ty_property .

types:
  begin of ty_object,
    properties type standard table of ty_property with empty key,
  end of ty_object .

types:
  begin of ty_objects,
    object      type standard table of ty_object with empty key,
    pathsegment type string,
  end of ty_objects .

types:
  begin of ty_table,
    objects         type standard table of ty_objects with empty key,
    hasmoreitems(1),
  end of ty_table .

 

 From the deserialization-Method I get lo_json_data as a deep structure.

The debugger shows me:

I  assign it to <data>. with  assign lo_json_data->* to <data>.

To show the structure of <data> I see the first level of the JSON-structure "OBJECTS"

When clicking on "OBJECTS" I get the second deep structure level "OBJECT":

The deep structure OBJECT again contains the deep structure PROPERTIES:

And the table PROPERTIES contains a undefined number of "basetypes" 

<DATA>-OBJECTS[1]-OBJECT-PROPERTIES

Every basetype is structured  with an ID, TYPE, CARDINALITY and a VALUE-Table, 

like the basetype %CSECONDARYOBJECTTYPEIDS as example.

<DATA>-OBJECTS[1]-OBJECT-PROPERTIES-%CSECONDARYOBJECTTYPEIDS

The VALUE can contain sometimes two ore more values:

<DATA>-OBJECTS[1]-OBJECT-PROPERTIES-%CSECONDARYOBJECTTYPEIDS-VALUE

I get stuck iterating to the bottom of the deep structures even though I know the structure is always the same.

<DATA>-OBJECTS[1]-OBJECT-PROPERTIES

Any hints or ideas?

entry.title: Kuok Group Singapore: An SAP BTP Adoption Journey with Migration of Workforce Mgmt Application
entry.content:
Kouk Group, one of Asia's most dynamic multinational conglomerates, is a leader in properties, logistics, hospitality, food, agribusiness and maritime. Kuok Group Singapore (the “Group”) is a conglomerate with diverse and global operations in maritime, real estate and digital infrastructure sectors.

This article narrates an adoption journey of SAP BTP, marking the collaboration between SAP and Kuok Group. The SAP team has been collaborating with Kuok Singapore to facilitate their transition of their applications and adoption towards SAP BTP. This includes demonstrating the potential of SAP BTP through enablement and proof of concept, with SAP BTP serving as the platforms for extensions for SAP S/4HANA Business Processes.

Use Case Scenario:

Currently Kuok Singapore uses a non-SAP application for workforce management in which HR personnel allocate projects for staffs based on their IN and OUT time. This process requires two tiers of approval before it initiates postings to SAP, generating project accounting P/L reports. This application was one of the selected use-cases to start their innovation and adoption journey on SAP BTP.

Solution:

Key considerations for building the proof of concept.

  1. Kuok Singapore wanted to assess the user experience of application that are developed using SAP Build Apps and SAP CAP.
  2. Evaluate SAP Build Process Automation for automating workflows and validations within the application.
  3. Options for seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA.
  4. Keep SAP BTP as the extension and integration platform for processes including SAP ERP business processes.

SAP’s Platform Architecture & Adoption team, part of the Office of the CTO, worked on accelerating the BTP adoption journey and developed a proof-of-concept for this business process utilising SAP Cloud Application Programming Model and SAP Build Process Automation to highlight the ease of development, user experience in line with SAP’s User Experience guidelines and seamless integration capabilities with SAP S/4HANA.

The SAP BTP Guidance Framework provides a central access point to build and run enterprise grade solutions on SAP BTP. The technical architecture has been developed based on this framework and this will be the guiding document for all future SAP BTP development in Kuok Singapore.

A high-level technical architecture of this scenario was as below:

 

 

Feedback from customer on the POC and way forward for SAP BTP Adoption:

In the recent SAP contract renewal from HEC to RISE, Kuok Singapore has purchased the SAP BTP cloud solution with CPEA Credits. This serves as the primary catalyst for quick innovations based on potential business use cases that enables us to fail fast and succeed faster. As at the time of writing this, we have already explored 3 business use cases on SAP BTP within a short span of 6-months, namely:

  1. Jobsheet Entry Sheet
  2. Material Reservation Form
  3. PR Request/Approval

With no skilled in-house technical resource, SAP CSP, Surya Lee worked with SAP BTP CSP, Rai Kamal Saha to collaborate with us to leverage on SAP`s BTP technical team expertise to build our first PoC on SAP BTP –  Job Sheet Entry. Uma Anbazhagan, the Platform Architecture and Adoption Lead worked quickly to assemble the technical team and shared with us the PoC project timeline and the architecture landscape.

The development team was agile and able to build and deliver the prototype based on our prototype requirements within 4 months timelines with a development effort of 8 weeks.

  1. A sleek, modernized and user-friendly UI via custom build application
  2. 2 layers of workflow approval via process build automation
  3. Seamless integration of Jobsheet entry posting in SAP S/4HANA

The prototype was indeed an eye-opener to what SAP BTP solution is capable of. It has proven to open the door of opportunities to explore potential business cases using SAP BTP technology.

SAP BTP’s seamless integration with SAP S/4HANA is a major advantage. The ability to build, extend, and innovate on top of SAP S/4HANA processes is simplified with prebuilt connectors, data models, and APIs. Most importantly, it ensures that business data remains stored exclusively in SAP S/4HANA (without being stored in the BTP layer), maintaining SAP S/4HANA as the single source of truth while preserving data integrity and security, says Jacqueline Beh from Kuok Singapore.

Next Steps and Plan Ahead

With the successful build of the Jobsheet prototype, the KGSg-SAP team gained confidence to propose to our fast-growing BU, K2 Data Centre to digitize their hardcopy PR/PO form request by leveraging on SAP BTP Solution. With the help of a new start-up company, Mark9, we managed to work with the Service Partner to build a prototype within 6 weeks to seek the successful buy-in of the BU. User was impressed with the modern and sleek but yet user friendly user interface.

Driven by business requirements, the project team works with business users and Service Partner to continuously fine tune the design solution. The integration capability of the BTP Cloud Connectivity enables the retrieval of PO Financial Authority Limit maintained in SAP S/4HANA and routing of PR/PO form for approval in BTP. Finally, the PR/PO form is integrated back as PO document creation/updates in SAP S/4HANA, as single source of truth for all POs.

With continuous support from SAP BTP expertise and our Service Partner, Mark9, Kuok looks ahead to digitize, automate and innovate in its next wave of SAP BTP adoption journey, says Jacqueline Beh from Kuok Singapore.

Credits

We would like to thank Kuok Singapore colleagues Jacqueline Beh, Siew Siew Toh, Prasanna Venkatesh Elumalai and Mohammed Vaseem. Their close collaboration in developing this proof of concept and subsequent steps on the SAP BTP Adoption Journey with SAP Build tools and services. On the SAP side, Many thanks to Rai Kamal Saha, Surya Lee for facilitating collaboration with all the stakeholders in this engagement, Mahesh Palavalli, Ajit Kumar Panda and Swati Maste for supporting the POC development and finally PVN Pavan Kumar, Anirban Majumdar for their leadership support.

Summary

Post the POC, the implementation will include integrations with the new HR system, integrations with SAP S/4HANA system and build additional approval scenarios with other validations and authorizations in place.

SAP Business Technology Platform offer the essential tools required to create, operate, and expand digital products. They also provide access to solutions and accelerators developed by their ecosystem, enabling businesses to easily innovate or source specific novel ideas to maintain agility. The SAP Business Technology Platform (SAP BTP) embodies this vision, serving its customers, partners, and SAP itself.

For an organization, who is getting started with SAP BTP Journey, this serves as a useful example which demonstrates how to leverage the platform to digitalize business operations, create straightforward automation scenarios, and integrate the SAP S/4HANA using the SAP BTP as an extension platform.

To learn more about SAP BTP, see the learning journey on SAP Learning called Discover SAP Business Technology Platform, a great introduction to BTP and the Intelligent Enterprise strategy to see what it’s all about for free or check out Develop Advanced Extensions with SAP Cloud SDK plus much more free learning at SAP Learning site.

To initiate your own adoption journey with us, please contact us on paa_india@sap.com.

entry.title: Hana B1 - Interactive analysis reports open blank documents or shows error
entry.content:
Hello,

We have a FP 2208 in a Hana system. When we try to open the Excel report and interactive analysis in the client the "Purchase analysis" report the excel does not show the pivot tables.

When we open directly the Excel report and interactive analysis app. We complete the log on information but we get an error message like "Data source initialization error".

The Excel report have been reinstalled.

Has this error appear to anyone?

Regards, Esti

entry.title: Request for Custom Field Editing in 'Create Billing Documents - F0798' Application in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We have encountered a recurring demand from our customers in Portugal for the ability to utilize multiple sales billing series (interval ranges) to accommodate invoicing customers for dates in the past. While this is relatively straightforward to implement in 'On-Premise' or 'Private Cloud' environments through the creation of custom billing document types, we have found it challenging in the 'Public Cloud.'

To work around this limitation, we created a custom field and custom logic using the Custom Field and Logic application, making it editable in the VF01 header data. We also implemented flexible numbering for billing documents. However, we still face challenges when attempting to achieve similar functionality through the 'Create Billing Documents - F0798' app, as it does not allow any data entry in the fields.

Is there any possibility of enabling custom fields to be editable/input in the F0798 application? This would greatly enhance our ability to meet customer needs.

Any insights or solutions would be greatly appreciated!

Thank you!

Bea

entry.title: Re: SAP Slipstream Engine: Keyboard opens automatically i...

entry.title: Consume REST SCIM API of SAP IAS
entry.content:
Hello

I'd like to create a simple user management API to consume the SCIM API of SAP IAS. 

I got as a result : <html><body>No service was found.</body></html>

Here are the steps I followed :

1 - creation of a destination in CF :

2 - setting my xs-app.json :

 

{
  "authenticationMethod": "route",

  "routes": [
    {
      "source": "^/api(.*)$",
      "target": "/api/$1",
      "destination": "IAS_Identity_Directory",
      "authenticationType": "xsuaa",
      "csrfProtection": false
    }
  ]
}

 

3 - updating the ui5-local.yaml :

 

# yaml-language-server: $schema=https://sap.github.io/ui5-tooling/schema/ui5.yaml.json

specVersion: "3.1"
metadata:
  name: com.vesi.zfamanage
type: application
server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertError: false  # Ignore certificate errors for local development
        backend:
          - path: /api
            destination: IAS_Identity_Directory
    - name: fiori-tools-appreload
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        port: 35729
        path: webapp
        delay: 300
    - name: fiori-tools-preview
      afterMiddleware: fiori-tools-appreload
      configuration:
        component: com.vesi.zfamanage
        ui5Theme: sap_horizon

 

4 - requesting the right path with a simple ajax call : 

 

        onInit: function () {
            this._fetchUsers();
        },

        _fetchUsers: function () {
            var sUrl = "/api/scim/Users";
            $.ajax({
                url: sUrl,
                method: "GET",
                contentType: "application/scim+json",
                dataType: "json",
                success: function (data) {
                    console.log("Users retrieved:", data);
                    MessageToast.show("Users retrieved successfully!");
                },
                error: function (err) {
                    console.error("Error fetching users:", err);
                    MessageToast.show("Error fetching users");
                }
            });
        }

 

I tried differents paths and approach without any success maybe I'm missing something on the connectivity part of BTP.
From Postman, I'm able to request the API.

Thank you in advance for your help! 🙂

Katharina

entry.title: Re: H2_2024 > SPOTLIGHT VIEW is missing username & report...
entry.content: for reports count, i've opened ticket 1148308/2024

entry.title: Attendance Collision Options
entry.content:
Hi,

We recently implemented Basic Timesheet for employees to capture overtime and a few other allowances. This is all duration based. The issue we have is that some employees will capture the same attendance type more than once in a day and this causes collision issues when replicating to ECP. I have tried to investigate if we could possibly configure some type of collision on the timesheet so that employees are not permitted to capture the same attendance type on a single day, but this does not seem to be possible. I wanted to find out if anyone else has encountered this and, if so, how it was resolved (if at all)?

Thanks,

Khotso

entry.title: Re: Setting up SAP Launchpad Service and SAP Mobile Start with Content Federation
entry.content:
Hi @pvanhoove , thanks for this blog.

I have completely configured SAP Mobile Start and it is working perfectly.

Today I take the Roles exposed from S/4 to BTP (through Tcode /n/UI2/CDM3_EXP_SCOPE) and link them to the Work Zone "Site Manager - Site Directory", and then I go to the Role Collection option in BTP and link these same Roles to the user with the "Use the Identity Provisioning service to provision user authorizations" option disabled:

 

Now I would like to know if the following advanced configuration is possible:

Now with the "Use the Identity Provisioning service to provision user authorizations" option enabled, the Roles are not replicated to the BTP "Rolle Collections".
Therefore, I would like to know if it is possible to make the roles automatically displayed to the end user just by adding the Roles in the Backend (S/4) in Tcode SU01/PFCG, without having to add them in BTP->Rolle Collection.

entry.title: Re: Service Procurement with spare parts
entry.content: Thanks, done!

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content:
Your binding syntax is correct.  You can reference expanded fields using the navigation property slash property name references. Based on your data retrieved by the query it should work. 

If you haven't already, I would suggest turning on odata tracing and debug odata provider to confirm the query sent and data retrieved matches your expectations.  If you are using a branded client you can turn it on in the BrandedSettings.json or if using an app generated from a template under the user menu at runtime.

entry.title: Re: Request for Custom Field Editing in 'Create Billing D...
entry.content:
HI Bea

According to the Fiori App library, extensibility options using SAPUI5 Adaptation projects are not possible using this app, please see the Implementation Information Tab at this link.

https://fioriappslibrary.hana.ondemand.com/sap/fix/externalViewer/#/detail/Apps('F0798')/S33

and feel free to create a customer influence request at the following link https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/2759/

Regards

entry.title: Re: Get Latest Performance Form in Story Report
entry.content: Hi, thank you for bringing this topic up. Do you know if there is any solution for filtering on the last form from different templates?

entry.title: Re: SAP S/4HANA Product Allocation Process
entry.content:
Hi Ruthvik

Thanks for a good explanation of PAL.

I am looking for a solution that somehow close the order lines that has exceeded the allocated qty withing a month. Meaning we do not want these orders to go in back order.  Unfortunately we cannot use the settings on the BP master as this is only relevant for some specific materials.

An example to understand the scenario:

Customer A, has a Product allocation for material B in November of 100 PC (In our business case this means that he can only bye 100 PC - Next month he might be allowed to order new 100 PC -  but then we request him to create a new order)

Order 1: Order qty 150 PC – Confirmed qty 100 PC (Due to Product allocation) – This gives us an open order of 50 PC  - And this is what we want to avoid

Order 2: Order qty 100 PC -  Confirmed qty 0 PC (Due to Product allocation – and the confirmed qty in order 1) Again – This gives us an open order of 50 PC  - And this is what we want to avoid

Do you have any ideas how we can solve this requirement ?

entry.title: Re: ABAP PLATFORM Standalone?
entry.content:
Please see the Blog on "ABAP Platform Trial Edition" : ABAP Platform Trial 1909 Available Now - SAP Community

Kind regards

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: SAP Development Reimagined: New Agile process with AI Tools, Clean Core, and Extensibility in SA
entry.content:
Really interesting subject! 
The possibilities ahead of us developers are fascinating.

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content:
No, you'll not see it in TOA01.

KPro and ArchiveLink are 2 distinct technos, KPro is not using TOA01.

Unluckily Fiori Apps are in most of the cases making use of KPro (with or without DMS) and therefore  not TOA0x tables anymore.

With HDM (Harmonized Document Management) SAP is re-introducing some ArchiveLink functionalities in some Fiori Apps as part of future SAP releases, but there is no information about in which App and when HDM will be available.

For some customers we have implemented some simple tools that allow to have attachments "synchronized" between ArchiveLink and Fiori KPro / DMS. Thanks to this attachments are only stored once physically, but are visible in SAPGUI, TOA0x and also in the Fiori Apps.

entry.title: Boleto de Concessionárias CNAB 240 - Segmento O
entry.content:
Olá experts

Estou configurando o segmento O no DMEE utilizando o layout BR_FEBRABAN_A, porém estou com dúvida a respeito do código de barras.

Pagamentos a concessionárias possuem código de barras de 48 posições, porém no SAP ECC temos o campo com somente 47 posições.

Existe alguma forma de converter as 47 posições em 48 através do cálculo dos dígitos? Ou seria possível criar uma tabela Z para alimentar os dados e parametrizar o atributo na DMEE para buscar dessa tabela. Vi também que existe a sugestão da SAP do uso do campo BSEG-SGTXT, mas não sei como codificar ou especificar para que tal campo seja lido.

Agradeço desde já pelas sugestões e ajuda.

#FEBRABAN

entry.title: Re: BAPI to see FB60 list

Please use the Search on the Developer Community or in the "Me for SAP" area, as there are many identical questions. One of many links : Solved: FB60 BAPI - SAP Community

Kind regards

Nic T.

entry.title: Re: Introduction for tcode MDS_LOAD_COCKPIT
entry.content: How can I synchronize a customer to a business partner with BP type "Person"? By default, SAP creates customers as BPs with BP type "organization".

entry.title: Re: SAP Learning Introduction
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano ,

The transition from FICO to Fiori was driven by the increasing number of projects requiring Fiori. Additionally, the older project I maintain are planning to upgrade to Fiori. This has motivated me to delve deeper into Fiori to better support both current and future projects.

Looking forward to sharing insights and learning from everyone here. Thanks for the warm welcome! I would greatly appreciate any guidance and tips you can share as I continue to explore Fiori.

entry.content:

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content: Thanks Karim. My requirement is unchecking KPRO in DMS, use Archive method configuration, store the document in archive server, those documents should visible in TOA01 table, especially in Fiori Apps.

entry.title: Update Site/Profit center in invoice when invoice is posted via EDI
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I have a requirement to enhance the invoice and update the site and profit center from Idoc that is posting it.

could you please suggest me the enhancement/user exits to achieve this... 

Main thing is I should be able to read idoc number in that exit.. I tried to update table DRSEG in one enhancement section, it did update site but not profit center.

please help

entry.title: Re: Automating SAP Post-Refresh Steps with SAP GUI Script...
entry.content:
Thank you for your feedback Jan. While SAP PCA offers structured automation through predefined task lists, it has limitations that SAP GUI scripting can address more effectively in specific scenarios. PCA task lists, although modifiable with variants, operate within a rigid framework that may not align with all business requirements. GUI scripting, on the other hand, offers greater flexibility and adaptability, especially for workflows outside PCA’s predefined scope.

Here are real-world examples where GUI scripting proved to be the superior approach:

  1. Customization for Unique Workflows:
    GUI scripting enabled automation for non-standard processes and custom SAP transactions with more ease. This ensured automation was tailored precisely to the client’s needs without compromising workflow. Although PCA supports custom T-code automation through variants and hooks, adapting or troubleshooting these configurations can be time-consuming and requires expertise. 

  2. Rapid Deployment and Time Savings:
    For smaller refreshes and urgent requirements, GUI scripting was deployed immediately, meeting critical deadlines without the setup overhead required for PCA configurations. In such cases, the speed of implementation was paramount.

  3. Cost-Effectiveness without Licensing Constraints:
    PCA requires SAP Landscape Management (LaMa) licenses, which can increase costs, particularly for non-production environments where the full suite of PCA functionalities may not be necessary​. On the other hand, GUI scripting leverages existing SAP tools without additional costs, making it cost-effective for clients looking to manage targeted automation without overhauling their setup. For my clients with non-production systems or limited automation needs, this avoided unnecessary costs while achieving the same end goals.

  4. Handling Ad-Hoc Changes on the Fly:
    In one recent project, time-sensitive configuration adjustments were required during a system refresh. Using GUI scripting, the team was able to immediately implement the necessary changes by automating inputs directly into the SAP GUI, without the need to modify or redeploy task lists. In contrast, modifying PCA’s predefined task list variants would have required additional configuration time and could have delayed the refresh process, jeopardizing project timelines.

  5. Lightweight Automation for Non-Core Tasks:
    For minor adjustments—such as user role updates, RFC configurations, or batch job modifications—GUI scripting provided a focused and streamlined solution without the need to invoke the entire PCA framework, making it far more practical for these use cases.

Moreover, GUI scripting enabled consistent, error-free execution, reducing manual effort while ensuring precision. The flexibility it offers makes it a superior alternative in certain business cases to PCA in environments that demand cost-effective, rapid, and custom automation solutions. While SAP PCA is highly effective for standardized post-copy activities, in practice, GUI scripting has complemented or even outperformed PCA, where quick customizations, ad-hoc adjustments, or cost considerations are priorities, delivering results more efficiently without unnecessary complexity.

Unless LaMa licenses and fully structured automation are essential, GUI scripting stands as the better option for businesses looking to automate workflows while managing costs and timelines effectively. It’s not just a theoretical preference—it’s a practical one, validated through successful implementations.

Best Regards,

Mohammed

entry.title: Re: Schedule publications SAC Stories in Excel file type
entry.content:
Hello Russell, 

This feature is already available in Fast Track Tenant (Version: 2024.21.0) and in QRC tenant it will be available in QRC4.2024 which is tentatively planned in Nov end 2024.

Thanks,
Pranav

entry.title: Re: New 2H 2024 SAP Successfactors Time (Tracking) Features
entry.content: @Volker_Ruof , can you please advise whether Time Tracking licenses are pre-requisite for the '1.13 Enhanced tracking possibilities of time sheet submit and approval'? This is a reporting functionality, not time tracking? Thank you very much

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content: Also, you have mentioned, some customers we have implemented with simple tool that allow to have attachments "synchronized" between ArchiveLink and Fiori KPRO/DMS - Is it completely new customization based on requirement? or GOS objects or Business documents attachments customized functionality? Could you please elaborate more?

entry.title: Re: SAP CAP - Setting up Multitenancy & using Extensibility for API directory
entry.content:
Hello @phips ,
SusaaS core contributor here 👋. Thank you very much for your interest in the repository!
Not using mtx/sidecar as a separate app was a design decision we took when we started developing the SusaaS sample.

The reason is, since it is not a big app, we did not need a separate application for just provisioning. There were 5-6 tenants expected for that application from the partner in a year. Since the application data model was not so big we did not have another workload under mtx/sidecar.

Which is the correct way to implement multitenancy?

If you want to have independent scalability, if you have a big data model and a complex application, I would strongly recommend to use the sidecar approach.
Otherwise, you can embed the sidecar into your main application, that was what we did for a smaller footprint.

How to use Hybrid Debug for a specific tenant in multitenancy?

You can get token on behalf of the tenant that you want to test and call the API's via client credentials. 
We also documented here the hybrid development here.

Is there a way to use Extensibility in API directory?
There was a very good reason for this from CAP side. @eckart_liemke can you please enlighten us? I do not know all the details of this.

In general if you have a feature request and bug report about the SusaaS sample, feel free to create issue on the repo, we appreciate any contribution.

entry.title: Re: Call Transaction CJ20n and jump on specific Milestone
entry.content: Thank you for your help! I think I did what you proposed. I copied Coding of Include LPSIS_SINGLE01F85 in my coding. In Include LPSIS_SINGLE01F86 I deleted Line 25 -> p_view_stack-vsnmr = <version>. because of course I couldnt feed the Field Symbol. After doing that, the SHARED BUFFER was correct and now it works 😉

entry.title: Join SAP Enterprise Support Day 2024 to Drive Your Business Transformation
entry.content:
We are pleased to invite you to SAP Enterprise Support Day, an exclusive virtual event designed to equip you with insights and strategies aimed at elevating your business transformation all through SAP’s comprehensive digital support experience.

Join us on November 13th or 14th, depending on your time zone and preferred language:

November 13, 2024 I 1 p.m. EST (Spanish, Portuguese, English) 

November 14, 2024 I 1 p.m. SGT (Chinese, Japanese, English)

November 14, 2024 I 1 p.m. CET (English, German)

A day filled with informative sessions and engaging interactive discussions is awaiting you:

  • Learn how SAP is transforming its own customer support through AI to enhance your business model
  • Discover AI-driven, Effortless Customer Support, transforming your experience with SAP solutions 
  • Explore strategies for optimizing cloud and hybrid business solutions with SAP Cloud ALM for effective Business Transformation
  • Understand how we help to simplify your procurement process with SAP Ariba implementation
  • Modernize your legacy ERP with RISE with SAP and SAP Enterprise Support
  • Master the acceleration of your SAP Business Technology Platform implementation
  • Discover how to drive business transformation with SAP Signavio and SAP LeanIX
  • Learn effective strategies to enhance your SAP Enterprise Support experience
  • Reimagine SAP HCM and enhance efficiency with AI-driven innovations

    Secure your spot by registering now and benefit from a day full of valuable insights.

    Register Now

entry.title: Re: How do we create a variable on WEBI that calculates i...

Hey, I don't know if you have resolved your problem. In any case, you could use a variable "Couple" where Couple is the concatenation of the 2 lab you search.

After that you could use your 60days measure where lab1+lab2 = "couple"...dunno if it help, without data samples can't answer correctly.

entry.title: B1iF Message Failure: Outbound FIE001 basePath or pattern is 'null' java.io.IOException
entry.content:
I have the following problem:

The customer’s SAP Business One system was updated to version 2408. Since then, a B1iF interface has stopped working. This interface exports invoice data as an XML file.

I have already checked the SLD entries in B1i. I also tested by setting the path to a local directory, and this works without any issues. However, the original path, which worked fine before the update, now throws the following error:

com.sap.b1i.xcellerator.XcelleratorException: XCE001 Nested exception: com.sap.b1i.xcl_file.FileAdapterException: FIE001 basePath or pattern is 'null' java.io.IOException: Das System kann den angegebenen Pfad nicht finden

entry.title: Re: INTRODUCTION TO GROUP REPORTING IN SAP S/4HANA (PART I)
entry.content: Nice article

entry.title: Re: Guide to use HANA NSE in the BW/4HANA system
entry.content:
Hello @AndreyF 
unfortunately, also @Dominik_Graus cannot change the Situation about the SAP IQ Setup, and it is a pity that so many customers are frustrated with the NLS/DTO implementation, as Q – the easy Installer for SAP IQ - ng 2024 never saw the real light at SAP. Nevertheless, I did so many Implementations in die past years with no issues at all.
Remember, if you activate NSE for SAP HANA, the data is stored back to disk which needs much more time to retrieve to the in-memory store. With that, the advantage of SAP HANA is gone at all ...

best Regards Roland

entry.title: Re: How to map the Archive server to Kpro in SAP S4 DMS
entry.content:
So you requirement is not possible in SAP standard.

For other customers, we have sold them our tool that allows to manage this. So this is not SAP standard but a SAP add-on, and not free of charge.

entry.title: Re: How to close the Scheduling Agreement
entry.content: What is the technical difference between blocking and deleting and when should we use the two?

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content:
Are you using Offline OData?

If you are using offline the other thing to verify is that the $expand entity set is included in the defining requests in your Initialize action.  When I was running a test I didn't have my $expand entity set defined so naturally got no data back for the expand.

You can set your "SecureDatabaseEncryptionKey": false in your BrandedSettings.json and then pull the offline db files from your simulator/emulator and open in ilodata directly to confirm what data in in the offline store.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content: Hi Bill, I'm using offline odata and it's not branded. I'm also checking everything you said and doing some analysis, I'll return asap.

entry.title: Re: How we connect SAP Build Apps with PostgreSQL.
entry.content:
Thanks for reaching out to us, 

This is not supported by Build Apps. There are no direct database connections that can be made from Build Apps applications. The only option to consume data from a postgres database is for the customer themselves to implement some backend service, and the expose OData/REST APIs from that service for consumption in Build Apps.

But you can Run and Deploy SAP CAP (Node.js or Java) with PostgreSQL on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/run-and-deploy-sap-cap-node-js-or-java-with-postgresql-on-sap-btp-cloud/ba-p/13558467

entry.title: Re: How Can I Connect a PostgreSQL Instance to SAP Build...
entry.content:
Thanks for reaching out to us, 

This is not supported by Build Apps. There are no direct database connections that can be made from Build Apps applications. The only option to consume data from a postgres database is for the customer themselves to implement some backend service, and the expose OData/REST APIs from that service for consumption in Build Apps.But you can Run and Deploy SAP CAP (Node.js or Java) with PostgreSQL on SAP BTP Cloud Foundry https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/run-and-deploy-sap-cap-node-js-or-java-with-postgresql-on-sap-btp-cloud/ba-p/13558467

entry.title: Where can I learn how to configurate and manage the Profile Worker functionality in SAP Fieldglass?
entry.content:
I recommend to access our Introducing Profile Worker in SAP Fieldglass learning journey


Overview
This learning journey will guide you through the configuration and management of the Profile Worker functionality in SAP Fieldglass, explaining its different functionalities and benefits.

Learning Objectives
Upon completing this course, administrators will be able to:
  • Explain the purpose of the Profile Worker functionality in SAP Fieldglass and its benefits
  • Configure the different aspects of the Profile Worker module based on their needs
  • Understand and manage the Profile Worker workflow process
  • Compare Profile Worker to Contingent Worker and Services Procurement functionalities

Prerequisites

  • Introducing SAP Fieldglass

Please ask a question related to the digital learning Journey in the Q&A area.

Our SAP Learning Experts will get back to you as soon as possible! We are here to support you. 

I appreciate your feedback and we will make sure to continue sharing interesting topics.

Kind regards
Margit

entry.title: Re: How to use joins in Webi Query Builder
entry.content:
You are correct, joins are not supported.  However, you might be able to use the "BI on BI" universe to get it.  If that doesn't work, you'll have to either look at a third-party tool - APOS and Wiisdom have good ones - or write code using either the Java or .NET SDK to get this info.

-Dell

entry.content:

I have complete the "Getting started with SAP HCM and HCM Payroll" course, but I am not sure about how to continue.

My specific interest is more in Personnel Administration, can you give me a clue?

Wich are the certifications most important? 

Thank you all in advance!

Agostina

entry.title: BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE not updating WBS element
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a development that requires me to create a new PR and update the WBS element based on user input. I am using the BAPI BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE to do the same.

The BAPI runs successfully and all fields are created/populated except for the WBS element.

Please find some of my code snippets below:

l_it_praccount TYPE STANDARD TABLE OF bapiebkn.   "Table Declaration

APPEND VALUE #( "res_doc = abap_false   "Populating Data
                      g_l_acct = <fs_deep>-sakto
                      preq_item = <fs_deep>-bnfpo
                      cost_ctr = <fs_deep>-kostl
                      wbs_elem = <fs_deep>-ps_psp_pnr   "WBS Element
                      distr_perc = <fs_deep>-vproz
                       )  TO l_it_praccount.

CALL FUNCTION 'BAPI_REQUISITION_CREATE'   "Passing Table to BAPI
            EXPORTING
              skip_items_with_error          = abap_true
              automatic_source               = abap_true
            IMPORTING
              number                         = lv_number
            TABLES
              requisition_items              = l_it_pritem[]
              requisition_account_assignment = l_it_praccount[]   "Table Mapping
*             REQUISITION_ITEM_TEXT          =
*             REQUISITION_LIMITS             =
*             REQUISITION_CONTRACT_LIMITS    =
*             REQUISITION_SERVICES           =
*             REQUISITION_SRV_ACCASS_VALUES  =
              return                         = lt_return[]
*             REQUISITION_SERVICES_TEXT      =
              requisition_addrdelivery       = l_it_item[]
*             EXTENSIONIN                    =
            .

Any ideas on where I might be going wrong?

Thank you.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK - Entities, Navigations and Fields
entry.content:
@bill_froelich 

I’m speechless... incredible! Here's what I did to solve the issue:

I went to downloadOffline.action and InitializeOffline.action and added the expand I needed. After doing that, the previously blank fields were populated with the expected data.

Now I can see the data in the Object Header.

Thanks a lot! I'm learning a lot with you and all community's posts and members.

entry.content:

As of now i used 24 copy operators to bring all months to the current month then have another new key figure to total all the actuals. I want to use the threshold in the XYZ Segmentation but I am having challenges in creating or choosing the right attribute. If you have any tips regarding this, please advise. Thank you so much

Regards,

Rafaelle

entry.title: SAP Build Apps and SAP S4HANA Cloud Public Edition
entry.content:
Hi, I have completed the mission Create an Application with SAP Build Apps, but I was wondering how can I connect to a SAP HANA  Cloud Public system, whether is a trial account or my company's dev tenant. 

Similar to the exercise in the mission, I would like to create an OData integration and connect to Overview | Outbound Delivery (A2X) | SAP Business Accelerator Hub  

When I enter the SANDBOX URL

https://sandbox.api.sap.com/s4hanacloud/sap/opu/odata/sap/API_OUTBOUND_DELIVERY_SRV;v=0002
and test the service, nothing happens...

Regards,

AxI

entry.title: SAP RAP: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT
entry.content:
Hello Community,

I need your help with one thing. 

I am currently implementing a “managed” RAP Fiori application and have encountered the following error in the course of my implementation:

 

Runtime Errors: BEHAVIOR_ILLEGAL_STATEMENT

Statement "CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK" is not allowed with this status.

The statements
- CALL FUNCTION IN UPDATE TASK
- CALL FUNCTION IN BACKGROUND TASK/UNIT
- PERFORM ON COMMIT/ROLLBACK
are not allowed outside of the "Save" method.


I don't have much experience in integrating existing SAP ABAP classes into my RAP application, as I have only been performing operations using EML. In this case and also in future transactions I will not always be able to avoid developing everything greenfield, so I have to use existing code.

Stupid question, but can someone explain the error to me exactly?
It seems that I can't write anything to the database outside of the “Save” method. Which can't always be avoided, especially if I use standard code.

How can this be prevented in the future? Do RAP applications then have to be implemented exclusively “unmanaged”? We haven't had much experience with this yet.

Many thanks for your help

regards,

Lars

entry.title: Re: EmployeeDataBundleMaintainRequest_sync - Flag MainBankIndicator is ignored
entry.content: Is there a solution for this?

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Data Exchange Launched as Generally Available!
entry.content:
Are you struggling to measure and assess your Scope 3 emissions to meet regulatory requirements and achieve your company's net-zero goal?
As a buyer, do you find it challenging to collect and manage product carbon footprint data from your suppliers in a simplified and standardized way?
Are you a supplier tired of filling out different questionnaires, excel sheets, and answering emails to deliver sustainability data to your customers? If so, SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is the solution tailored to meet your needs.

What is SAP Sustainability Data Exchange?

In short, SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is a Software-as-a-Service solution based on SAP BTP that facilitates standardized data requests and sharing of Scope 3 emissions between supply chain partners. It also assists organizations in managing their own product carbon footprint data as well as that of their business partners.

What makes it different?

What makes SAP Sustainability Data Exchange different? It is all about standardization and network collaboration.

The cloud solution is integrated into SAP Business Network to leverage Trading Partner Directory and search capability for common registration, profile, and invitation process. SAP Sustainability Data Exchange is industry agnostic, supports global standards as well as industry framework such as WBCSD PACT and Catena-X. It is officially listed as certified app for product carbon footprint exchange for Catena-X data space.  

Additionally, through out-of-the-box integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, organizations can truly realize carbon accounting on a transactional level, moving from averages to actuals.

Curious and want to learn more?

Visit SAP.com/SDX  for more exciting product information. We are now supporting digital sales experience! You can also find out more detailed guides on Help Portal and the future capabilities on the Roadmap Explorer.

Partners can also access the latest assets on the Partner Portal.

See how our reference customer WITTE Automotive talk about their decarbonization journey with SAP Sustainability solutions. 

Thank you to our customers, partners, colleagues, and the core SAP Sustainability Data Exchange team that have been and continue to contribute to its success! 👏 💚

**************************

Hi, 👋 I'm Leyi Liu, have been working with SAP Sustainability Data Exchange since almost the very beginning, now since October 2024 officially as Product Marketing Lead. I'm looking forward to sharing more updates and news on the product with you in the future. Stay tuned!

entry.title: Re: Request for Custom Field Editing in 'Create Billing D...
entry.content: Many thanks chicagonz! Could I ask you if this requirement that can be developed by the partner or this is product limitation that cannot be done. Thanks in advance! Bea

entry.title: Odata - Lock table entries till DEQUEd explicitely
entry.content:
Hi All,

I am using OData protocol and my requirement is table entries are to be locked unless explicitly DEQUEd  by FM. Since OData is stateless protocol , lock is retained till control exists in backend. 

I guess in GUI we used achieve this by using SCOPE = 1 . Can anyone please confirm if we can still achieve using ENQUE FMs.

Below blog seems to be a solution but not sure if it can cause any performance issues or any other limitations

https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/sap-odata-soft-state-with-delayed-invocation-for-locking/ba-p/13467989

entry.content:
Hi @DellSC , I am able to find that Universe , In my case could you please help me with the filter criteria & list of the Columns name which I need to bring into output ?

I need list of Webi Report names Vs BEX Query name

entry.title: I want to start my Career In SAP Sourcing and procurement
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

My Name is Hassan. I want to start my career in SAP Sourcing and procurement because i have a background of supply ChainManagement. I know that through SAP Learning i can gain knowledge regarding the interested Module.

entry.title: Unable to Transport OIM Account
entry.content:
Hi Team

 

We are unable to transport the OIM changes for GL Account 

Account balance is equal to zero, but the account contains postings


Diagnosis
You have changed the Open Item Management option (SKB1-XOPVW). However, the general ledger account contains postings for the current fiscal year, the prior year, or the year preceding the prior year.
Procedure
Changing the setting for this option merely changes account control. Items posted up to this date are not automatically switched to the changed setting.
If this option is subsequently activated, the existing line items are not converted to 'open items'.
We therefore recommend that you proceed as follows:
Use the job template Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts in program Schedule Accounting Data Changes to retroactively change the indicator for open item management. For more information, 
Message no. FH190


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Unable to Transport OIM Account
entry.content:
In the long text of error  FH190

You are recommended 
"Use the job template Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts in program Schedule Accounting Data Changes to retroactively change the indicator for open item management"

This is the solution. 

See also kba 3023775 - Error FH190 occurs when trying to change the open item management indicator

See help.sap.com documentation 

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0fa84c9d9c634132b7c4abb9ffdd8f06/658b0f8061424109936672e336a1bb0f.html?locale=en-US

 

Why can't I change the open item management setting in development system. 

First, it depends which kind of open item management you use. Different methods are possible for open item management, open item management by ledger group, and extended open item management. For example, for open item management, when you try to save a change to the Open Item Management indicator in the Manage G/L Account Master Data app, the development system communicates with the production system to check if it’s possible to make such a change. The production system checks and makes sure that the affected account has a zero balance and no postings. If the systems determine that the change is allowed, it is included in the next transport from the development system to the production system. If the systems determine the change isn’t allowed, you must schedule a job in the Schedule Accounting Data Changes app using the Activate Open Item Management for G/L Accounts job template. For more information about all three kinds of open item management, see Open Item Management for G/L Accounts.

entry.title: Re: 🟢 Transforming legacy code to CAP with the help of GenAI
entry.content:
@gregorw  For SHINE Application following are the tokens. Subjective to change in future as we plan to incorporate some LLM tasks on the converted CAP code for further optimisations.

Input Tokens Used: 518000
Output Tokens Used: 152000
